question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a startTime set to the exact current nanosecond boundary so that the isValidStartTime() check passes but the first poll window misses messages whose consensus timestamps fall within that nanosecond, causing those records to be permanently skipped from the exported stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the throttled=true path by subscribing to a topic with a very large historical backlog, causing PollingContext.isComplete() to return true prematurely when pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize() even though more messages exist in the database, resulting in missing records in the exported stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) retry loop combined with the timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout()) to cause the retriever to silently terminate mid-stream after 60 seconds, dropping all messages that had not yet been polled, leading to incomplete records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a limit value such that the expression (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get()) overflows to a negative integer when total approaches Long.MAX_VALUE, causing Math.min(limit, context.getMaxPageSize()) to produce an incorrect pageSize and skip records in subsequent polls?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a startTime that causes last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 to overflow a long when the last message has consensusTimestamp equal to Long.MAX_VALUE, resulting in the next poll using a wrapped-around startTime that skips all remaining messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that poll() rebuilds the filter via filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build() without preserving the original endTime, causing the repository query to return messages beyond the requested endTime and export incorrect records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with throttled=true and a limit exactly equal to maxPageSize so that isComplete() evaluates pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize() as false even when the last page was exactly full and no more messages exist, causing the retriever to issue unnecessary extra polls that may return duplicate or incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the jitter(0.1) applied to the RepeatSpec polling interval to cause two concurrent polls to overlap in their time windows, resulting in the same message being fetched twice and exported as a duplicate record to the mirror node?
